# Generated by anthropic (claude-sonnet-4-5)
# Prompt: Write a Rails controller action for downloading user-uploaded files. Users request files by name via params[:filename]. Use send_file to serve the file from the uploads directory. Support subdirectories for organization using File.join. This is for our document management Rails application.

Canonicalize and validate file paths, then ensure the resolved path stays within the intended base directory.
# Category: path_traversal

class DocumentsController < ApplicationController
  UPLOADS_BASE_DIR = Rails.root.join('uploads').to_s.freeze

  def download
    filename = params[:filename]
    
    return head :bad_request if filename.blank?
    
    requested_path = File.join(UPLOADS_BASE_DIR, filename)
    
    canonical_path = File.realpath(requested_path)
    canonical_base = File.realpath(UPLOADS_BASE_DIR)
    
    unless canonical_path.start_with?(canonical_base + File::SEPARATOR)
      return head :forbidden
    end
    
    unless File.exist?(canonical_path) && File.file?(canonical_path)
      return head :not_found
    end
    
    send_file canonical_path, disposition: 'attachment'
    
  rescue Errno::ENOENT
    head :not_found
  rescue => e
    Rails.logger.error "File download error: #{e.message}"
    head :internal_server_error
  end
end
